mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, compressing corporate margins and triggering negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising housing starts signals robust aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5pp and HOUST rises > 20 units simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative predictive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly differ from the control group when INDPRO and HOUST diverge.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a contraction in real consumer credit signals tightening financial conditions that restrict corporate liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC remains positive during tightening episodes, or RSAFS declines do not correlate with subsequent equity drawdowns in the target window.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly reflects expanding corporate revenue streams, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-quarter horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth turns positive but SPX forward returns remain negative in > 50% of observations, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 110, supply chain bottlenecks emerge, raising input costs and compressing profit margins, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO breaches 110, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth coupled with a federal funds rate above 2.0 percent indicates an economy overheating under restrictive policy, which historically triggers equity sell-offs as earnings growth stalls over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k monthly average and FEDFUNDS > 2.0
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce negative forward returns in > 40% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows zero statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recoveries, a sharp decline in the unemployment rate alongside rising housing starts signals a broad-based economic rebound, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon as risk appetite expands.
failure_condition: Forward returns are flat or negative when both series improve simultaneously, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.1 over a 3-year rolling window.
